mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative or flat (within 0.1%) in more than 50% of periods where RSAFS month-over-month growth is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5.0% signals a tightening labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and potential Federal Reserve tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances when UNRATE is below 5.0% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy relative to price pressures. This environment typically supports equity valuations by keeping discount rates low, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2% and FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) during periods of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) suggests stagflationary pressures. This combination erodes corporate profits and consumer purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of periods where INDPRO is declining and CPIAUCSL 12-month growth is above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong economic confidence and future construction activity. This typically boosts overall market sentiment and signals future economic growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of instances where HOUST shows strong 3-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS), reflects a healthy labor market and robust consumer spending capacity. This fundamental strength supports corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) increase the cost of capital for businesses and reduce the present value of future earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has started hiking rates in late 2015/early 2016
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where FEDFUNDS has increased by more than 0.25% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong but retail sales growth (RSAFS) is weak, it suggests a divergence where overall economic activity is not translating into robust consumer spending. This indicates potential future weakness for consumer-facing companies, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where GDPC1 growth is strong but RSAFS growth is weak.
